The **Battle of Steenbergen**, also known as the **Capture of Steenbergen of 1583**, took place on 17 June 1583 at [Steenbergen](/wiki/Steenbergen "Steenbergen"), [Duchy of Brabant](/wiki/Duchy_of_Brabant "Duchy of Brabant"), [Spanish Netherlands](/wiki/Spanish_Netherlands "Spanish Netherlands") (present-day [North Brabant](/wiki/North_Brabant "North Brabant"), the [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands")). This was an important victory for the Spanish [Army of Flanders](/wiki/Army_of_Flanders "Army of Flanders") led by Don [Alexander Farnese, Prince of Parma](/wiki/Alexander_Farnese,_Duke_of_Parma "Alexander Farnese, Duke of Parma") ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *Alejandro Farnesio*), [Governor-General of the Spanish Netherlands](/wiki/List_of_governors_of_the_Habsburg_Netherlands "List of governors of the Habsburg Netherlands"), over the combined army of Dutch and French led by the French Marshal [Armand de Gontaut, Baron de Biron](/wiki/Armand_de_Gontaut,_Baron_de_Biron "Armand de Gontaut, Baron de Biron"), and the English commander Sir [John Norreys](/wiki/John_Norris_(soldier) "John Norris (soldier)"), during the [Eighty Years' War](/wiki/Eighty_Years%27_War "Eighty Years' War"), and in the context of the [French Wars of Religion](/wiki/French_Wars_of_Religion "French Wars of Religion").[[1]](#cite_note-Mack_P._Holt_p._190-1)[[2]](#cite_note-James_Tracy._''The_Founding_of_the_Dutch_Republic:_War,_Finance,_and_Politics_in_Holland_1572–1588''-2) The victory of the Spaniards ended the [Treaty of Plessis-les-Tours](/wiki/Treaty_of_Plessis-les-Tours "Treaty of Plessis-les-Tours"), and [Francis, Duke of Anjou](/wiki/Francis,_Duke_of_Anjou "Francis, Duke of Anjou") *([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *François de France*)*, left the Netherlands in late June.[[5]](#cite_note-Holt_p._191-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Mack_P._Holt_p._192-6)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cc/SteenbergenMiddeleeuwen.jpg/330px-SteenbergenMiddeleeuwen.jpg)](/wiki/File:SteenbergenMiddeleeuwen.jpg)

Panoramic view of [Steenbergen](/wiki/Steenbergen "Steenbergen"). Unknown artist.

After the failure of the combined army of Dutch and French led by Marshal [Armand de Gontaut, Baron de Biron](/wiki/Armand_de_Gontaut,_Baron_de_Biron "Armand de Gontaut, Baron de Biron"), put in charge of the army by [Prince William of Orange](/wiki/William_the_Silent "William the Silent") ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): *Willem van Oranje*), to [relieve the city of Eindhoven](/wiki/Siege_of_Eindhoven_(1583) "Siege of Eindhoven (1583)"),[[7]](#cite_note-Holt_p._190-7) the French commander moved his army to the north of [Roosendaal](/wiki/Roosendaal "Roosendaal"), between [Breda](/wiki/Breda "Breda") and [Bergen op Zoom](/wiki/Bergen_op_Zoom "Bergen op Zoom"), and after several days of siege, captured the castle of [Wouw](/wiki/Wouw "Wouw") on 10 May.[[5]](#cite_note-Holt_p._191-5)[[8]](#cite_note-James_Tracy._''The_Founding_of_the_Dutch_Republic''-8) Meanwhile, the Prince of Parma, with the city of Eindhoven insured, continued his advance across [North Brabant](/wiki/North_Brabant "North Brabant").[[8]](#cite_note-James_Tracy._''The_Founding_of_the_Dutch_Republic''-8) Farnese moved with part of his army to [Namur](/wiki/Namur,_Belgium "Namur, Belgium"), while the rest of the Spanish troops, commanded by General [Karl von Mansfeld](/wiki/Karl_von_Mansfeld "Karl von Mansfeld"), captured the towns of [Turnhout](/wiki/Turnhout "Turnhout") and [Hoogstraten](/wiki/Hoogstraten "Hoogstraten"), defeating the small Dutch garrisons,[[3]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Don_Hernando_de_Acosta-3) and finally, the city of [Diest](/wiki/Diest "Diest") on 27 May.[[9]](#cite_note-Graham_Darby_p._20-9)

From his base at [Namur](/wiki/Namur,_Belgium "Namur, Belgium"), the Prince of Parma advanced to capture the town of [Herentals](/wiki/Herentals "Herentals"), but the siege was finally abandoned, and Parma continued to advance to confront the forces of Marshal Biron located near [Roosendaal](/wiki/Roosendaal "Roosendaal").[[5]](#cite_note-Holt_p._191-5) With the news of the Spanish advance, Biron moved his army to the outsides of [Steenbergen](/wiki/Steenbergen "Steenbergen"), between the same town and [Bergen op Zoom](/wiki/Bergen_op_Zoom "Bergen op Zoom"), and on 17 June, with the arrival of the forces of Parma the battle began.[[3]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Don_Hernando_de_Acosta-3)[[10]](#cite_note-D._J._B._Trim_p._253-10)

## Battle and capture of Steenbergen

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battle_of_Steenbergen_(1583)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Battle and capture of Steenbergen")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/59/Vaenius_-_Alexander_Farnese.png/250px-Vaenius_-_Alexander_Farnese.png)](/wiki/File:Vaenius_-_Alexander_Farnese.png)

Governor-General Don [Alexander Farnese](/wiki/Alexander_Farnese,_Duke_of_Parma "Alexander Farnese, Duke of Parma") by [Otto van Veen](/wiki/Otto_van_Veen "Otto van Veen").

Despite their numerical superiority, the combined forces led by Biron and Sir [John Norreys](/wiki/John_Norris_(soldier) "John Norris (soldier)"), who commanded the [English](/wiki/English_people "English people") [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry")[[11]](#cite_note-11) along with the [Welsh](/wiki/Wales "Wales") Captain [Roger Williams](/wiki/Roger_Williams_(soldier) "Roger Williams (soldier)"),[[12]](#cite_note-Nolan_p._47-12) the just officer who put some order in defending the Spanish attack, were literally swept away by the Spanish [cavalry](/wiki/Cavalry "Cavalry"), and then by the infantry,[[3]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Don_Hernando_de_Acosta-3) suffering heavy losses, thanks also to the bombardment success of the [guns](/wiki/Cannon "Cannon") of Don Hernando de Acosta, [Lieutenant-General](/wiki/Lieutenant-General "Lieutenant-General") of the [artillery](/wiki/Artillery "Artillery") of the [Army of Flanders](/wiki/Army_of_Flanders "Army of Flanders"), who had a very important role in the battle.[[3]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Don_Hernando_de_Acosta-3)[[7]](#cite_note-Holt_p._190-7) The forces of Biron and Norreys suffered at least 3,200 casualties, and almost all the baggage, barrels of gunpowder, 36 flags, and 3 banners captured.[[3]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Don_Hernando_de_Acosta-3) On the Spanish side the casualties were 400 dead or wounded.[[7]](#cite_note-Holt_p._190-7) The French Marshal himself, who tried to repel the attack of the Spaniards, fell from his horse and broke his leg.[[13]](#cite_note-Jan_Wagenaar._''Vaderlandsche_Historie._Volume_7_p._489-13)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5d/Armand_de_Gontaut.jpg/250px-Armand_de_Gontaut.jpg)](/wiki/File:Armand_de_Gontaut.jpg)

French Marshal [Armand de Gontaut, Baron de Biron](/wiki/Armand_de_Gontaut,_baron_de_Biron "Armand de Gontaut, baron de Biron"), by Thierry Bellange.

The combined army was virtually destroyed and retreated in disarray to the [fortress](/wiki/Fortress "Fortress") of Bergen op Zoom and other towns controlled by the Dutch rebels, and the Spanish troops took with little opposition Steenbergen.[[3]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Don_Hernando_de_Acosta-3)[[13]](#cite_note-Jan_Wagenaar._''Vaderlandsche_Historie._Volume_7_p._489-13) A few days later, the lack of pay and the differences between the [French](/wiki/French_people "French people") soldiers (mostly [Catholics](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic")) and the [Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_people "Dutch people") and English [Protestant](/wiki/Protestant "Protestant") troops (even the differences between the Dutch and English commanders), ended with hundreds of desertions among Biron's troops.[[14]](#cite_note-Kamen2-14)[[15]](#cite_note-Mack_P._Holt_p._191-15)

## Consequences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Battle_of_Steenbergen_(1583)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Consequences")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a2/Workshop_of_Michiel_Jansz._van_Mierevelt_005.jpg/250px-Workshop_of_Michiel_Jansz._van_Mierevelt_005.jpg)](/wiki/File:Workshop_of_Michiel_Jansz._van_Mierevelt_005.jpg)

Prince [William of Orange](/wiki/William_the_Silent "William the Silent") by [Michiel Jansz. van Mierevelt](/wiki/Michiel_Jansz._van_Mierevelt "Michiel Jansz. van Mierevelt").

The result of the battle was an overwhelming Spanish victory, not only in terms of casualties in favour of the Spaniards, also in terms of immediate strategic consequences.[[10]](#cite_note-D._J._B._Trim_p._253-10) The position of [Francis, Duke of Anjou](/wiki/Francis,_Duke_of_Anjou "Francis, Duke of Anjou"), became impossible to hold with the [States-General of the Netherlands](/wiki/Dutch_Republic "Dutch Republic"), and he eventually left the [Low Countries](/wiki/Low_Countries "Low Countries") in late June.[[5]](#cite_note-Holt_p._191-5)[[6]](#cite_note-Mack_P._Holt_p._192-6) The defeat and the end of the [Treaty of Plessis-les-Tours](/wiki/Treaty_of_Plessis-les-Tours "Treaty of Plessis-les-Tours") was a severe blow to the Dutch Protestants and discredited [William of Orange](/wiki/William_the_Silent "William the Silent"), his main supporter.[[6]](#cite_note-Mack_P._Holt_p._192-6) Moreover, the Spanish progress was unstoppable, and the Prince of Parma moved to [Dunkirk](/wiki/Dunkirk "Dunkirk"), a city blocked by the forces of the Spanish commander Don [Cristóbal de Mondragón](/wiki/Crist%C3%B3bal_de_Mondrag%C3%B3n "Cristóbal de Mondragón").[[16]](#cite_note-Jeremy_Black_p._110-16) On 16 July the bombardment began, and a few days later, the city surrendered to the Spaniards, along with [Nieuwpoort](/wiki/Nieuwpoort,_Belgium "Nieuwpoort, Belgium") on 23 July.[[8]](#cite_note-James_Tracy._''The_Founding_of_the_Dutch_Republic''-8)[[16]](#cite_note-Jeremy_Black_p._110-16)

The Spanish army continued its advance, and after the possibility of putting under siege the port city of [Ostend](/wiki/Ostend "Ostend"), the plan was abandoned, and the bulk of the army advanced to [Diksmuide](/wiki/Diksmuide "Diksmuide"), that capitulated on 1 August.[[17]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Hernando_de_Acosta-17) Meanwhile, a Spanish detachment also captured [Veurne](/wiki/Veurne "Veurne") and [Menen](/wiki/Menen "Menen").[[17]](#cite_note-Biography_of_Hernando_de_Acosta-17)
